palette_hint: off-white background (#f7f5f0), dark slate text (#2c3240), warm amber primary accent (#e8a020), muted teal secondary accent (#2a7d6f)
type_style: Georgia serif for headings (bold, editorial feel), system sans-serif (Arial/Helvetica) for body and UI elements at regular weight
description: Marketing site for Inkwell, a SaaS platform that turns messy internal knowledge bases into clean, searchable, auto-organized documentation hubs for mid-size engineering teams.
constraints: centered container max-width 1080px with 24px horizontal padding on all pages
top nav: Inkwell wordmark in serif bold on the left, page links in small-caps sans-serif in the center, amber 'Start Free Trial' button on the right with 6px border-radius
footer: 3-column grid with brand blurb on left, site links in middle, contact info on right, separated from content by a 2px teal top border
section headings use serif at 36–48px; body copy uses sans-serif at 16px with 1.6 line-height
plan cards and feature cards use a 1px solid #ddd border with 10px border-radius and 24px internal padding

page_specs.home: Full-width hero section with a large serif headline ('Stop Losing Knowledge. Start Building Clarity.'), a two-sentence subheadline about turning scattered docs into organized wikis, and two side-by-side CTA buttons (amber 'Start Free Trial' and teal-outlined 'See How It Works'). Below the hero, a 3-column grid showcasing three core benefits: Auto-Organization, Smart Search, and Team Permissions — each with an emoji icon, bold title, and two lines of description. A final narrow banner section displays a single customer quote in italic serif with the author's name and company.
page_specs.features: Page opens with a centered heading 'Everything Your Team Needs' and a short two-sentence intro paragraph. Below, an alternating two-column layout presents four features in detail: each row has a colored rectangular placeholder on one side and feature title plus three-sentence description on the other, alternating left-right. Features covered: AI Tag Suggestions, Version History, Slack Import, and Role-Based Access.
page_specs.pricing: Centered heading 'Simple, Transparent Pricing' followed by a billing toggle label (Annual / Monthly, rendered as plain text with a note that annual saves 20%). Three plan cards in a CSS grid row — Starter ($0/mo), Growth ($49/mo), and Scale ($149/mo) — with the Growth card highlighted by a 2px amber border and a small 'Most Popular' badge in amber at the top. Each card lists 5 feature bullets, a price, and a CTA button.
page_specs.integrations: Intro heading 'Connects With Tools You Already Use' with a one-sentence description. A 4-column CSS grid displays 8 integration tiles, each a bordered box containing the integration name in bold and a one-line description (e.g., 'GitHub — sync repo READMEs automatically', 'Notion — import pages in one click'). Below the grid, a short two-sentence callout section with teal background invites teams to request a custom integration via a plain link.
page_specs.contact: Two-column layout: the left column contains a contact form with fields for Full Name, Work Email, Team Size (a select dropdown with options 1–10, 11–50, 51–200, 200+), Message textarea, and an amber submit button labeled 'Send Message'. The right column displays the support email address, a phone number, office location (Portland, OR), and average response time, each preceded by a small emoji icon for visual separation.
